definitions.technical: The complete set of documents, data, and reasoning that a federal agency relies on when issuing a regulation. Courts confine their review to this record when evaluating whether the agency acted within its statutory authority.
definitions.lay: The full collection of evidence and explanations a government agency uses when creating a new rule. If the rule is challenged in court, the judge looks at this record to decide whether the agency acted reasonably.
definitions.parenthetical: (the complete documentary basis for an agency's rulemaking decision)
definitions.legal_significance: Under the APA (5 U.S.C. Section 706), courts review agency action based on the administrative record. A thin or poorly constructed record increases the likelihood that a rule will be vacated as arbitrary and capricious.
